---
title: "Section 2: Import, Visualize, and Communicate Assignment"
author: "My Ha"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

## From the Student Locations Google Drive, load files to your Working Directory:

Data332_Section_2_Data.xlsx and an image ggplot_layers.png

<https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1g6OOSGXJnF3Q97MBD9BT1M4JmPLfJ6rd>

## References

ggplot(): <https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/html/data-visualization.html>

## ![Figure 1: GGPLOT Layers](ggplot_layers.png)

Cheat Sheet : <https://rstudio.github.io/cheatsheets/data-visualization.pdf>

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document.

To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org.>

## Libraries Needed

```{r}
library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
```

```{r}
library(tibble)
library(purrr)
library(stringr)
library(readr)
library(readxl)
library(writexl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```

## Read in the data to your session

```{r}
section_2_Data<- read_xlsx("Data332_Section_2_Data.xlsx")
```

## Check the datatypes of trending_table

```{r}
table_datatypes <- tibble( column = names(section_2_Data),
type = map_chr(section_2_Data,
~ paste(class(.x), collapse = ", ")) )
print(table_datatypes)
```

## Line Charts

Produce a line chart of Revenue by Order Date

```{r}
Line_Chart <- ggplot(section_2_Data, aes(x = Order_Date, y = Revenue)) +
geom_line(color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 month") +
labs( title = "Revenue Over Time", x = "Order Date", y = "Revenue" ) +
theme_minimal()+
theme( axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust = 1) )
print(Line_Chart)
```

```{r}
ggsave("Line_Chart.png", width = 20, height = 8, dpi = 300)
```

## Horizontal Bar Charts

```{r}
# Summarize quantity by product
product_qty <- section_2_Data %>%
  group_by(Product) %>%
  summarise(Total_Quantity = sum(Quantity, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  arrange(Total_Quantity)

# Horizontal bar chart
bar_chart <- ggplot(product_qty, aes(x = Total_Quantity,
                                     y = reorder(Product, Total_Quantity))) +
  geom_col(fill = "lightblue") +
  labs( title = "Total Quantity Sold by Product",
        x = "Total Quantity", y = "Product" ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) + # smaller overall font
  theme( axis.text.y = element_text(size = 8), # smaller y‑axis labels
         plot.title = element_text(size = 12) # adjust title size
         )+
         coord_cartesian(clip = "off")
  print(bar_chart)
```

```{r}
ggsave("bar_chart.png", width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

## Histogram of Profit

```{r}
Profit_histogram <- ggplot(section_2_Data, aes(x = Profit)) +
geom_histogram(
bins = 30, # adjust bin count as needed
fill = "darkgreen",
color = "white",
alpha = 0.8
) +
labs(
title = "Distribution of Profit",
x = "Profit",
y = "Frequency"
) +
theme_minimal()
print(Profit_histogram)
```

```{r}
ggsave("Profit_histogram.png", width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

## Use of Categorical Variable in a Bar Chart

```{r}
# Summarize count of orders by survey response
survey_counts <- section_2_Data %>%
group_by(Order_Survey_Response) %>%
summarise(Count_Orders = n()) %>%
arrange(desc(Count_Orders))
# Vertical bar chart
Order_by_survey_response <- ggplot(survey_counts,
aes(x = Order_Survey_Response, y = Count_Orders)) +
geom_col(fill = "orange") +
geom_text( aes(label = Count_Orders),
vjust = -0.5, # moves labels above the bars
)+
  labs(
    title = "Count of Orders by Survey Response",
x = "Survey Response",
y = "Number of Orders"
) +
theme_minimal() +
theme(
axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1) # rotate labels
)
print(Order_by_survey_response)
```

```{r}
ggsave("Order_by_survey_response.png", width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

## Box Plots

```{r}
box_plot_Cost <- ggplot(section_2_Data, aes(x = Cost_per_Unit)) +
geom_boxplot(fill = "steelblue", color = "black") +
labs(
title = "Boxplot of Cost per Unit",
x = "Cost per Unit",
y = ""
) +
theme_minimal() +
theme(
axis.text.y = element_blank(), # remove y‑axis labels
axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), # remove y‑axis ticks
axis.title.y = element_blank() # remove y‑axis title
)
print(box_plot_Cost)
```

```{r}
ggsave("box_plot_Cost.png", width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

## Violin Graph of Profit by Order Survey Response

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
Violin_survey <- ggplot(section_2_Data, aes(x = Order_Survey_Response,y = Profit)) +                      geom_violin(fill = "steelblue", color = "black", alpha = 0.7) +
labs(
title = "Profit Distribution by Survey Response",
x = "Survey Response",
y = "Profit"
) +
theme_minimal() +
theme(
axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1) # rotate labels
)
print(Violin_survey)
```

```{r}
ggsave("Violin_survey.png", width = 8, height = 10, dpi = 300)
```

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content

and the output of embedded code.

You can embed code like this: The echo: false option disables the printing of code (only

output is displayed).
